summary: End-to-end testing of Product Variants feature: backend (variant CRUD, by-barcode lookup, order creation/edit/delete/restore stock reconciliation, OOS, regression) + frontend (admin Products variant editor, admin OrderNew variant select, barcode-scan→variant auto-add, agent mobile new-order). 13/14 backend tests pass. 1 backend bug found in PATCH /orders/{id} that does NOT decrement variant stock on edit. All admin-facing UI variant flows look correct visually and functionally.

critical.issue: When editing an order whose item has a variant_id, the new-item decrement loop omits variant_id, so it decrements parent product.stock instead of variants.$.stock. The OLD-item restock loop (and create_order/restore) correctly pass variant_id; only the edit decrement does not. Net effect: variant stock is OVER-restored on edit (only added back, never re-deducted). Reproduced via test_patch_qty_reconciles_variant_stock — expected variant stock = before-newQty, observed = before. RCA: server.py around line 1002 calls apply_stock_change(...) without variant_id. Fix: replace inline loop with `await _apply_decrements(new_items, order['number'], current)` to mirror create_order, OR pass variant_id=it.get('variant_id'). Same `reason='order_edited'` should be preserved.

issues: Missing data-testid on agent-add-line button correctly named 'agent-add-line' (not 'agent-add-line-button'). Variant Select inside agent line item has no data-testid (admin equivalent is 'line-variant-{idx}'). Recommend adding 'agent-line-product-{idx}' and 'agent-line-variant-{idx}' for symmetry & testability.

verified_flows: POST /api/products with variants[] persists labels/sku/barcode/price/stock; GET /api/products returns them.
GET /api/products/by-barcode/{code} returns {product, variant} for variant SKU/barcode and {product, variant: null} for parent SKU.
POST /api/orders without variant_id on a variant-product → 400 'has variants — choose one'.
POST /api/orders with variant_id → unit_price = variant.price, items[*].variant_label set, variant.stock decremented, parent.stock untouched.
Tier pricing (parent-level) overrides variant base price when qty meets min_qty (12 → $4.50 instead of variant $3.50).
Out-of-stock guard returns 400 with 'Name · Label (have X, need Y)'.
DELETE then restore correctly reverses variant stock.
Plain product (no variants) regression — order, list still works without variant_id.
FRONTEND admin: Products dialog shows variant grid (label/sku/barcode/price/stock + remove). Product list row shows 'from $X.XX' and 'N variants · M tiers' summary.

FRONTEND admin: /admin/orders/new shows second variant Select after product chosen with 'Label · N left' options. Submitting without variant shows toast 'Product has variants — choose one'. Summary and OrderDetail show 'Product · Variant'. Stock decremented on backend.
FRONTEND scan: BarcodeScanner manual entry of variant barcode auto-adds line with variant pre-selected and toast 'Added: Product · Variant'.
action_items: FIX backend PATCH /api/orders/{id}: pass variant_id when decrementing new items on edit (server.py ~line 1002). Recommended one-liner: replace inline loop with `await _apply_decrements(new_items, order['number'], current)` and consider also passing reason='order_edited' through _apply_decrements (or keep the loop but include `variant_id=it.get('variant_id')`).
(Minor) Add data-testid='agent-line-product-{idx}' and 'agent-line-variant-{idx}' on AgentNewOrder.jsx for parity with admin OrderNew.jsx and easier automation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py update_order inlines the decrement loop instead of reusing _apply_decrements helper, leading to the variant_id omission bug. DRY/consistency violation — the same pattern is correctly handled via helpers in create_order and the restore endpoint.

Variant pricing semantics intentional & correctly implemented: customer custom_price (parent-level) > tiered (parent-level, qty-based) > variant.price > product.base_price. Documented behaviour matches request spec.

Frontend Products.jsx uses crypto.randomUUID() to generate variant ids client-side — server still regenerates via Variant.id default factory on insert; both fine, but new ids are server-trusted only on create. On edit (variants list update), client-supplied ids are persisted as-is — recommend backend regenerate ids only when missing to avoid collision.

success_rate.frontend: 100% admin flows verified; agent flow partial (functional in code, no data-testids to automate)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST Premium Cola product with 2 variants (1 L @$3.50/100, 2 L @$6.50/50) and TEST Plain Item (no variants) and TEST VarCust customer. Several TEST orders left in DB — cleanup not strictly required (soft-deletable).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the PATCH order variant_id bug at server.py ~line 1002, rerun: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_variants.py::TestVariantOrderPatch -v. Backend tests are idempotent and create their own TEST_ data each run.
rca of the issue: PATCH /api/orders/{id} bug: line 1002 calls `apply_stock_change(it['product_id'], -qty, 'order_edited', order['number'], current)` without `variant_id=it.get('variant_id')`. Helper signature defaults variant_id=None which routes the $inc to product.stock instead of variants.$.stock. Reproduction: create order with variant qty=3, PATCH to qty=5; observed variant.stock back to original (only old restocked, new not decremented at variant level). Fix: add variant_id kw or call _apply_decrements(new_items, ...) which already passes it correctly.
